author: @theAxe6
text:
What kind of magnetic properties does the graphene has? 
Is it diamagnetic like graphite? More or less? ...if you tested it on magnetism. 
I just had an idea of replacing all repeling forces in electric motors vith diamagnetic forces by using graphene coils somehow.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I am very interested in resonant cavity fuel cell. At the moment i am hung up on graphene - but only for it's uses in free energy research and because there is a thing called graphetised carbamide which is a catalyst for water splitting and i am wondering if we could use that too to further reduce the energy input required. that's sort of what i am working on at the moment. for those reasons

author: @theAxe6
text:
I would like to see the same experiment with distilled water. 
I know that electrolysis should not happen if there are no electrolytes in the water, but maybe we don't need them with such high voltage. If nothing else the amper draw should be reduced. 

Is it possible to break the bonds in the water molecule by some kind of resonance? 
If you are interested google: "resonant cavity water fuel cell", i would like to hear your opinion on this matter.

replies.author: @navneetpandit7299
replies.text: It  works with Distilled water too☝️☝️

author: @iluzyon1
text:
lol you are right, building an engine out of this would take a lot of engineering and time. Plus, i am not sure it would be economical :) .

Make sure not to stand in front of that plunger when you try it or you wont be able to do the video of it! I cant wait to see that. :)

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: My thoughts exactly. There are two things i plan to try - one is to reduce the power consumption and the second is to enclose it and see if i can shoot a plunger up into the air - the jump to an engine isn't too great from there. I am off to sweden for a few days though and as keen as i am to try this it will have to wait - i wil share when i get back and have tried it out

author: @iluzyon1
text:
Wow! That is really cool! I have no material to try it at home and i was very curious to see the result. Thank's for trying and sharing it. 

- Enclosed in a cylindre with piston over it, it could become a HHO powered engine lolllll

Very good work!
